scope.claim: Evidence-backed conformance for SwiftQL's existing public SQLite syntax and adopted behavior surface; not complete SQLite grammar coverage.
limits: SQLite's reviewed syntax documentation is a dimension checklist, not an executable grammar.
Only public SwiftQL constructors named by feature records are in scope.
Supported syntax requires deterministic rendering and successful preparation by a version-identified real SQLite engine.
Representative execution proves selected semantics; it does not enumerate every legal SQLite interaction.
Planned suites and deferred features carry no invented evidence and are not counted as supported.

sqlite_environments.toolchain: Xcode 26.5 (17F42); Apple Swift 6.3.2 (swiftlang-6.3.2.1.108 clang-2100.1.1.101); macOS SDK 26.5; GRDB 6.29.3

features.title: INTEGER affinity converts numeric text
schema_requirements: A real table column declares the target SQLite affinity and exposes typeof after insertion.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; behavior rewritten against SwiftQL public contracts; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Extends declared INTEGER coverage with real SQLite coercion and typeof evidence.

features.title: TEXT affinity converts integer input

features.title: Repeated named binding identity

features.title: Nested transaction or savepoint capability boundary
schema_requirements: A transaction-owned table records stable logical row IDs before and after the operation.
deviations: The v1 driver has no nested transaction or savepoint hook.

deferral.reason: Issue #113 owns the required GRDB adapter and transaction capability hooks.

features.title: Single-connection visibility capability boundary

deviations: The v1 GRDB adapter accepts a DatabasePool, not a single-connection writer.

features.title: Pinned immutable Northwind correctness corpus
schema_requirements: The immutable northwind.db resource matches SHA-256 cb6f0071a264e150d3796f75c4b0643e32b2132e4e02370518b50a1eac3381d8, passes PRAGMA integrity_check, and exposes exactly 13 application tables, 17 views, 93 customers, 830 orders, 2,155 order-detail rows, and 77 products.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT-licensed database copied byte-for-byte as an immutable test artifact; the verbatim license notice is retained and no upstream Swift harness was copied.
provenance.adaptation_notes: The northwind.fixture.pinned-corpus case uses the checksum-pinned artifact cb6f0071a264e150d3796f75c4b0643e32b2132e4e02370518b50a1eac3381d8 and compares typed SwiftQL results with an independent raw-SQL or packaged-view oracle.

features.title: UNION and UNION ALL over compatible typed rows
schema_requirements: Both compound branches expose compatible typed row layouts.

features.title: Direct scalar compound rows without SQLScalarResult wrappers
schema_requirements: A stable one-column scalar row layout and alias are required across every branch.
deviations: Current compound APIs rebuild row metadata through XLResult and require a wrapper for scalar rows.

deferral.reason: Issue #43 owns direct scalar compound and CTE row support.

features.title: INTERSECT and EXCEPT real-SQLite prepare coverage
schema_requirements: Both compound branches expose compatible typed row layouts.

features.title: MATERIALIZED and NOT MATERIALIZED CTE hints
schema_requirements: A typed CTE definition with an explicit materialization policy is required.
deviations: SwiftQL does not currently expose SQLite materialization hints.

deferral.reason: Issue #10 owns materialized and non-materialized common tables.

features.title: Named and recursive common table expressions
schema_requirements: Recursive branches expose a stable alias, compatible columns, and a terminating predicate.

features.title: CREATE TABLE and CREATE TABLE AS SELECT
schema_requirements: Generated table metadata supplies names and SQLite storage declarations.
deviations: This record covers the existing create-table surface, not a complete typed SQLite DDL model.

features.title: Typed dialect-aware constraints, indexes, alter, and drop model
schema_requirements: Stable dialect-aware table, column, constraint, index, and schema-object metadata are required.
deviations: The existing public DDL surface is centered on generated CREATE TABLE statements.

deferral.reason: Issue #139 owns the typed dialect-aware DDL foundation.

features.title: Current INSERT, INSERT SELECT, UPDATE, and DELETE statement clauses
schema_requirements: A generated writable table supplies insert, update, and delete metadata.
deviations: Conflict algorithms, UPSERT, and RETURNING are separate deferred sub-surfaces.

features.title: INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE RETURNING clauses
schema_requirements: DML statements need typed result-row and cardinality metadata.
deviations: The current DML statement protocols are write-only and do not expose RETURNING rows.

deferral.reason: Issue #57 is the first atomic RETURNING implementation; #53 and #58 cover DELETE and UPDATE.

features.title: COUNT, MIN, MAX, SUM, AVG, and GROUP_CONCAT aggregate expression constructors
schema_requirements: Aggregate input columns use compatible numeric, comparable, or text expression types; nullable-result constructors preserve empty-input semantics.
deviations: Real-SQLite evidence covers every aggregate family on populated, empty, and all-NULL input plus GROUP_CONCAT DISTINCT and separator variants; the generated matrix adds a typed COUNT projection, while remaining DISTINCT and deprecated nonoptional combinations stay deferred.

deferral.reason: Issue #286 owns exhaustive real preparation and execution combinations for DISTINCT and the remaining aggregate overloads.

features.title: IIF, COALESCE, and simple and searched CASE expression constructors
schema_requirements: Conditional branches resolve to compatible SwiftQL result and optionality types.
deviations: The SQLite 3.32.0 minimum is set by IIF; CASE and COALESCE are available in older releases.

features.title: Prepared bitwise NOT, unary negation, equality, NULL predicate, text concatenation, LIKE, IN-list, and collation expressions
schema_requirements: Column references, literals, and bindings use the exact compatible SwiftQL expression and optionality overloads named by this record.
deviations: Support is limited to the named and evidenced constructors; Boolean combinators, comparison ordering, remaining arithmetic, GLOB, and query-backed IN overloads are recorded separately as partial.

features.title: UNIXEPOCH date conversion constructors and subsecond modifier
schema_requirements: Date inputs are ISO-8601 text expressions or constant strings accepted by SQLite's date parser.
deviations: UNIXEPOCH is typechecked, rendered, prepared, and executed in the generated suite; exhaustive subsecond modifier and optional-result combinations remain deferred.

deferral.reason: Issue #76 owns exhaustive subsecond modifier, version-gating, and optional-result checks.

features.title: JSON_ARRAY_LENGTH and JSON_VALID expression constructors
schema_requirements: JSON inputs are SQLite text expressions; path overloads require a valid SQLite JSON path string.
deviations: The generated suite prepares and executes representative JSON_VALID and JSON_ARRAY_LENGTH cases, but the pinned environment record does not yet attest the JSON-function capability, so the feature remains capability-gated.

deferral.reason: Issue #286 owns explicit JSON capability attestation and exhaustive preparation and result checks for both constructors.

features.title: LIKE expressions with an explicit ESCAPE clause
schema_requirements: A typed text expression, pattern, and single-character escape expression are required.
deviations: Current LIKE construction does not expose SQLite's ESCAPE clause.

deferral.reason: Issue #21 owns the LIKE ESCAPE clause.

features.title: ABS, ROUND, FLOOR, and scalar MIN and MAX expression constructors
schema_requirements: Arguments satisfy the public numeric or comparable SwiftQL expression constraints.
deviations: Representative ABS, ROUND, FLOOR, and scalar MIN cases are typechecked, rendered, prepared, and executed; exhaustive overload and optionality combinations, including scalar MAX execution, remain deferred.

deferral.reason: Issue #286 owns exhaustive capability-aware overload and optionality combinations for numeric and comparable scalar functions.

features.title: Remaining public Boolean, comparison, arithmetic, GLOB, and optional operator overloads

public_api.symbol: Remaining integer operators and optional overloads (-, *, /, %)
prefix numeric + and optional numeric overloads (+, -)
XLExpression.glob and optional text +/like/glob overloads (+, like, glob)

schema_requirements: Operands satisfy the public SwiftQL numeric, Boolean, comparable, text, and optional type constraints.
deviations: The generated suite adds prepared and executed arithmetic-precedence and GLOB cases to the existing renderer checks.
Floating-point, optional, comparison, and remaining Boolean and arithmetic overloads do not yet have exhaustive real-SQLite prepare coverage.

deferral.reason: Issue #287 owns exhaustive render, prepare, and execution coverage for every remaining public operator overload.

features.title: PRINTF string expression constructors
schema_requirements: PRINTF arguments are SwiftQL expressions accepted by SQLite formatting.
deviations: PRINTF is typechecked, rendered, prepared, and executed with named bindings in the generated suite; exhaustive overload and formatting combinations remain deferred.

deferral.reason: Issue #286 owns exhaustive real-SQLite PRINTF overload, formatting, binding, and result combinations.

features.title: Typed INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, and BLOB CAST expression constructors
schema_requirements: Only the public Bool, integer, real, text, data, and optional overloads are in scope.
deviations: Boolean-to-integer conversion is represented by the stored expression without an emitted CAST; all other exposed conversions render SQLite CAST expressions.
Text-to-BLOB and text-to-INTEGER semantics are proven by real SQLite; the remaining cast directions do not yet have an exhaustive conversion-result matrix.

deferral.reason: Issue #286 owns exhaustive real-SQLite prepare and conversion-result cases for every exposed cast direction.

features.title: INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, and CROSS JOIN with typed table references
schema_requirements: Outer-joined rows use nullable metadata and ON predicates use typed Boolean expressions.
deviations: NATURAL, USING, RIGHT, and FULL joins are excluded from this supported sub-surface.

features.title: NATURAL JOIN and JOIN USING
schema_requirements: USING needs typed shared-column identity; NATURAL JOIN needs an explicit overlap policy.
deviations: Join constraints currently use ON expressions or no constraint for CROSS JOIN.

deferral.reason: Issue #45 owns NATURAL JOIN and USING.

features.title: Typed SELECT projections with FROM, WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT, and OFFSET
schema_requirements: Selected row metadata and table references belong to the same query construction scope.
deviations: This record does not claim every SQLite SELECT branch or every aggregate form.

features.title: Typed ascending and descending ORDER BY terms
schema_requirements: Ordering terms wrap a typed expression in a SELECT ORDER BY clause.

features.title: COUNT(*) projection
schema_requirements: A typed all-columns expression must render as an unqualified asterisk.
deviations: Current count helpers require an explicit expression and do not expose count(*).

deferral.reason: Issue #17 owns an all-columns expression and COUNT(*).

features.title: Correlated scalar subqueries
schema_requirements: The inner query has a typed row reader and may reference a visible outer table dependency.
deviations: The registered real-SQLite evidence covers a correlated scalar aggregate; table and IN-subquery preparation remain separate.

features.title: Nullable-table and direct-scalar subquery result shapes
schema_requirements: Nullable table references and scalar cardinality must preserve typed result metadata.
deviations: The current overload set cannot represent every nullable-table and scalar result shape.

deferral.reason: Issue #70 owns nullable-table and scalar subquery shapes.

features.title: Table and IN-subquery real-SQLite prepare coverage
schema_requirements: The subquery exposes a compatible typed row or scalar layout to the enclosing query.
deviations: The generated suite prepares typed table-subquery branches; exhaustive table and IN-subquery overload combinations remain deferred.

deferral.reason: Issue #288 owns exhaustive real-SQLite prepare and execution coverage for table and IN-subquery overloads.

features.title: Fluent model soft-delete, restore, force-delete, and default-filter lifecycle
features.not_sqlite_syntax_reason: Soft-delete filtering and restore semantics are ORM record-lifecycle policy, not SQLite syntax.
schema_requirements: Fluent model lifecycle metadata, default query scopes, and restore semantics would be required.
deviations: SwiftQL is a typed SQL construction library and does not implement ORM soft-delete lifecycle behavior.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; behavior reviewed and intentionally excluded; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: The model, query, timestamp, bulk, force-delete, restore, and eager-parent subcases remain visible as deliberate ORM-scope exclusions.

features.title: Writes are visible inside the pinned transaction connection
schema_requirements: A transaction-owned table records stable logical row IDs before and after the operation.

features.title: Committed writes are visible after pool reentry
schema_requirements: A transaction-owned table records stable logical row IDs before and after the operation.

features.title: Rolled-back writes remain absent after pool reentry
schema_requirements: A transaction-owned table records stable logical row IDs before and after the operation.

features.title: First positive demand reads the current initial value
schema_requirements: A stable ordered query exposes the current database state when the subscriber first requests positive demand.

features.title: Each subscriber receives a fresh current value
schema_requirements: Two subscribers observe the same ordered request before and after a committed update.

features.title: Rapid relevant commits remain serialized and monotonic
schema_requirements: A single tracked row receives a deterministic series of committed values followed by a liveness write.

features.title: Writes outside the tracked region do not expose changed state
schema_requirements: The fixture contains a tracked table and an unrelated table in the same database.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; behavior rewritten as a SwiftQL observation-region contract; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Uses SwiftQL's typed request and a committed unrelated-table write to prove the durable observed snapshot remains unchanged.

features.title: Rolled-back relevant writes never appear
schema_requirements: A relevant write is rolled back before a later committed liveness write reaches the same observation.

features.title: A multi-write transaction publishes only durable state
schema_requirements: One transaction performs multiple relevant writes whose intermediate states are distinguishable from the final state.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; transaction-observation behavior independently adapted; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Uses SwiftQL's transaction path and asserts that no intermediate multi-write state is delivered.

features.title: Transient busy failures retry within the fixed budget
schema_requirements: A deterministic SQLite function injects busy failures into a real observation attempt.

features.title: Permanent observation failures terminate without retry
schema_requirements: A typed non-null row decoder reads a real SQLite NULL to produce a permanent decoding failure.

features.title: Zero demand starts no fetch and delivers no value
features.not_sqlite_syntax_reason: Downstream demand is a Combine publisher contract, not SQLite syntax.
schema_requirements: A manual subscriber can withhold demand while a real observed table changes.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; demand behavior independently adapted to SwiftQL; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Uses a SwiftQL request publisher and asserts logger-backed zero-fetch behavior before first demand.

features.title: Incremental demand bounds delivery and later reads current state
features.not_sqlite_syntax_reason: Finite downstream demand is a Combine publisher contract, not SQLite syntax.
schema_requirements: A manual subscriber requests one value at a time around a committed observed-table update.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; demand behavior independently adapted to SwiftQL; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Uses a SwiftQL request publisher to prove one unit of demand cannot deliver two snapshots.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; demand behavior independently adapted to SwiftQL; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Requests a later unit of demand and checks the newly fetched current state rather than an undemanded stale snapshot.

features.title: Cancellation suppresses every later delivery
features.not_sqlite_syntax_reason: Publisher cancellation is a Combine and adapter lifecycle contract, not SQLite syntax.
schema_requirements: The fixture can cancel before demand, after an initial value, and while real SQLite work is in flight.

provenance.license_disposition: MIT; cancellation race behavior independently adapted to SwiftQL; no source copied
provenance.adaptation_notes: Covers cancellation before demand and during blocked SQLite work, with a fresh observation proving pool liveness afterward.

features.title: Observation delivery stays attributed to its database pool
schema_requirements: Two database files expose identical schemas through distinct observed pools and an unrelated writer.
